name: Miso Soup with Tofu and Mushrooms
steps: Bring vegetable broth to a gentle simmer in a saucepan.
Whisk a ladle of hot broth into the miso paste until smooth, then stir it back into the pot.
Add cubed tofu and sliced mushrooms; warm through without letting it boil.
Drizzle with sesame oil and top with scallions before serving.

name: Nasu Dengaku (Miso-Glazed Eggplant)
steps: Slice eggplant into thick rounds and brush with sesame oil.
Broil or pan-sear the eggplant until golden and tender, a few minutes per side.
Whisk miso paste with a splash of water into a thick glaze.
Brush the glaze over the eggplant and broil briefly until bubbling.
Scatter with scallions and sesame seeds before serving.

name: Miso-Ginger Salmon Donburi
steps: Cook brown rice according to package directions.
Whisk miso paste with grated ginger and a little water into a glaze.
Brush salmon with the glaze and broil 8-10 minutes until cooked through.
Saute bok choy in sesame oil until just wilted.
Serve salmon and bok choy over brown rice, topped with scallions.

name: Dubu Jorim (Korean Braised Tofu and Zucchini)
steps: Slice tofu into thick planks, pat dry, and sear in sesame oil until golden.
Add garlic, gochugaru, and broth to the pan and bring to a simmer.
Nestle sliced zucchini around the tofu and simmer, spooning sauce over, until tender.
Top with scallions before serving.

name: Daegu-Jorim (Gochugaru-Braised Cod)
steps: Whisk garlic, ginger, gochugaru, and broth into a braising sauce.
Warm sesame oil in a skillet and lay in the cod fillets.
Pour the sauce over the fish, cover, and simmer 8-10 minutes until it flakes easily.
Garnish with scallions and serve.

name: Gochugaru Chicken and Corn Rice Bowl
steps: Cook brown rice according to package directions.
Season diced chicken thighs with gochugaru and garlic; sear in sesame oil until cooked through.
Add corn and cabbage to the pan and stir-fry until just tender.
Serve over brown rice.

name: Goong Pad Makham Wan (Thai Tamarind Shrimp Stir-Fry)
steps: Whisk tamarind paste with a splash of water into a tangy sauce.
Heat sesame oil in a wok and stir-fry garlic until fragrant.
Add shrimp and bell peppers; stir-fry until the shrimp turn pink.
Pour in the tamarind sauce and toss to coat; garnish with scallions.

name: Tamarind Turkey Stir-Fry with Green Beans
steps: Heat sesame oil in a skillet and brown the ground turkey, breaking it up as it cooks.
Stir in garlic and ginger and cook until fragrant.
Add green beans and stir-fry until crisp-tender.
Whisk tamarind paste with a splash of water and toss through to coat.

name: Nam Khao Tod (Crispy Rice Salad with Pork)
steps: Sear pork tenderloin until cooked through, then rest and slice thin.
Toss puffed rice with shredded cabbage, scallions, and mint.
Add the sliced pork and squeeze lime juice over the top.
Sprinkle with red pepper flakes and toss just before serving so the puffed rice stays crisp.

name: Shrimp & Herb Rice Paper Rolls with Tamarind Dipping Sauce
steps: Cook rice noodles according to package directions, then drain and cool.
Dip a rice paper sheet in warm water until pliable.
Layer lettuce, shrimp, noodles, carrots, cucumber, and mint near one edge.
Fold in the sides and roll tightly; repeat with the remaining wrappers.
Whisk tamarind paste with a little warm water into a dipping sauce and serve alongside.

name: Edamame & Herb Rice Paper Rolls with Miso-Sesame Dipping Sauce
steps: Steam or thaw shelled edamame until tender.
Dip a rice paper sheet in warm water until pliable.
Layer lettuce, edamame, carrots, cucumber, and mint near one edge.
Fold in the sides and roll tightly; repeat with the remaining wrappers.
Whisk miso paste with sesame oil and a little warm water into a dipping sauce.

name: Cantonese-Style Crab and Sesame Rice Noodle Bowl with Crispy Fox Nuts
steps: Cook rice noodles according to package directions, then drain and cool.
Toast fox nuts in a dry pan until crisp, then set aside.
Toss noodles with julienned cucumber and carrots, sesame oil, and scallions.
Top with crab meat, toasted fox nuts, and sesame seeds before serving.
